mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth, compressing corporate margins and depressing equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production combined with rising retail sales indicates robust aggregate demand outpacing supply constraints, driving earnings revisions upward and lifting equity prices over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns show no significant spread between high-demand/strong-supply regimes and baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing construction activity acts as a high-frequency proxy for consumer credit expansion, generating positive equity returns over a one-week horizon when the macro environment reflects post-pandemic reopening.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are flat or negative during housing-led demand surges, or the signal loses predictive power when GDP growth exceeds 3.5% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the CPI exceeding 0.5 percent signals accelerating inflation expectations, prompting rate hike fears that compress equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive despite CPI spikes above threshold, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below 50% in a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid employment growth coupled with surging retail sales indicates a self-reinforcing demand-labor feedback loop that boosts corporate revenues and lifts equity prices over a one-quarter horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns do not exhibit a statistically significant spread between high-employment/high-sales regimes and baseline, or the interaction term fails to reject the null hypothesis over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained industrial production growth reflects expanding capacity utilization and operational efficiency, directly supporting earnings per share growth and driving positive equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in production growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 3.0 percent signals robust economic momentum that historically precedes positive equity returns over a one-week horizon as earnings revisions accelerate.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative forward returns in > 40% of instances where GDP growth exceeds the threshold, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 55% over a 3-year test period.
